title: Review: ws.rs/store.rs/daemon/relay/mls round 28
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — drain() message-loss race and daemon MLS reconnect failure are the most dangerous; both affect operational correctness silently. PoW retain and find_addr blocking SQLite will only appear at scale.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: MLS state transitions under reconnect have complex interactions (nie-ztc6.6 may have additional failure modes beyond the 'group already exists' error); store.rs drain() race window is narrow and I have not traced all callers of enqueue() to verify the concurrent-write case is actually possible during drain. Would raise confidence if: integration tests cover reconnect-during-drain and concurrent enqueue+drain scenarios; also want to verify nie-tb8e.7 (MLS lock held across from_slice) was actually fixed in current code.
